title: Position-neighbor undercut pressure
summary: Undercut/overcut decisions depend heavily on nearby rivals, traffic, and whether pitting creates or avoids a strategic threat.
rationale: Undercut/overcut decisions depend heavily on nearby rivals, traffic, and whether pitting creates or avoids a strategic threat. Prior field medians are broad; the stronger signal may be adjacent cars by track position on the same lap.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Year-LapNumber, sort by Position and add features comparing the current driver to position ahead/behind: TyreLife delta, degradation delta, compound mismatch, LapTime_Delta delta, and whether neighbor just pitted or is on a new stint. Add min/median stats for top-3 nearest positions rather than the whole field.
expected_effect: Could improve ranking for cars likely to pit defensively or attempt an undercut, especially around dense mid-race windows.
risk: Position may not equal physical gap, and synthetic/test data may not preserve full grids; missing neighbors need robust defaults.
